request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

hypothesis.intervention: replace sucrose with trehalose as the cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than the standard DMSO protocol
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All six core fields are explicitly stated in the hypothesis with a concrete intervention, named cell line, defined comparator, named outcome, numeric threshold, and a biological mechanism.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose DMSO cryopreservation HeLa viability trypan blue
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

references.title: Trehalose-based cryopreservation of mammalian cells
references.relevance_note: Covers trehalose as a cryoprotectant in mammalian systems.

literature_qc.explanation: Papers using trehalose as a cryoprotectant exist in the literature, primarily in the context of cell preservation and lyophilization. No paper was found combining trehalose with HeLa cells specifically using trypan blue viability as the primary endpoint.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

risks.mitigation: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

plan.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with trehalose versus standard DMSO protocol.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be divided into two groups: one group will be cryopreserved using trehalose as the cryoprotectant, while the other will use DMSO. Each group will undergo the same freezing and thawing protocols, followed by viability assessment using trypan blue staining. The experiment will be replicated three times to ensure reliability of results.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved with 10% DMSO in freezing medium.
HeLa cells without any cryoprotectant in freezing medium (control group).

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 72 h | Cell Culture Preparation
Culture HeLa cells in DMEM (Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium) supplemented with 10% FBS (Fetal Bovine Serum) and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator until they reach 80% confluency (approximately 48-72 h).
Equipment: Thermo Fisher Scientific CO2 Incubator, T25 Cell Culture Flask
Reagents: DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092), 10% FBS (Gibco, 10099-141), 1% Penicillin-Streptomycin (Gibco, 15140-122)
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluency.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Cryoprotectant Preparation
Prepare two cryoprotectant solutions: 10% trehalose (w/v) in DMEM for the experimental group and 10% DMSO (v/v) in DMEM for the control group. Filter-sterilize both solutions using a 0.22 µm filter.
Equipment: Sterile 50 mL Centrifuge Tubes, 0.22 µm Filter Unit
Reagents: 10% Trehalose (Sigma, T9531), 10% DMSO (Sigma, D2650), DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092)
Expected outcome: Cryoprotectant solutions are sterile and prepared.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA solution. Incubate for 5 min at 37°C. Neutralize trypsin with an equal volume of complete DMEM and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 Centrifuge, T25 Cell Culture Flask
Reagents: 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056), Complete DMEM (prepared previously)
Expected outcome: Cells are detached and pelleted.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Cryopreservation
Resuspend the cell pellet in either 10% trehalose or 10% DMSO solution at a density of 1 × 10^6 cells/mL. Aliquot 1 mL into cryovials and freeze at -80°C in a controlled rate freezer (CoolCell LX) at a rate of -1°C/min until reaching -80°C, then transfer to liquid nitrogen for long-term storage.
Equipment: CoolCell LX Freezing Container, Cryovials (Nunc, 374202)
Reagents: 10% Trehalose solution, 10% DMSO solution
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully frozen and stored.

DAY 3 | 5 min | Thawing Cells
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and immediately place in a 37°C water bath. Thaw cells rapidly until just a small ice crystal remains (approximately 2-3 min).
Equipment: 37°C Water Bath
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed rapidly with minimal ice remaining.

DAY 3 | 30 min | Post-thaw Recovery
Transfer thawed cells to 10 mL of complete DMEM in a 10 cm culture dish and incubate at 37°C for 30 min to allow recovery.
Equipment: 10 cm Culture Dish
Reagents: Complete DMEM (prepared previously)
Expected outcome: Cells are allowed to recover for 30 min.

DAY 3 | 15 min | Viability Staining
After recovery, collect cells and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min. Resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of PBS and stain with 100 µL of 0.4% trypan blue solution for 5 min at room temperature.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 Centrifuge, Hemocytometer
Reagents: 0.4% Trypan Blue (Gibco, 15250-061), PBS (Phosphate Buffered Saline)
Expected outcome: Cells are stained and ready for counting.

DAY 3 | 20 min | Cell Counting
Count viable (unstained) and non-viable (stained) cells using a hemocytometer. Calculate the percentage of viable cells based on the total cell count.
Equipment: Hemocytometer
Expected outcome: Viability percentage is calculated.

DAY 3 | 30 min | Data Recording
Record the viability percentages for both trehalose and DMSO groups. Repeat the entire experiment two additional times to ensure replicability of results.
Expected outcome: Data is recorded for analysis.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Statistical Analysis
Conduct statistical analysis using a two-tailed t-test to compare the viability percentages between the trehalose and DMSO groups. Set significance level at p < 0.05.
Equipment: Statistical Software (e.g., GraphPad Prism)
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined.

DAY 3 | 30 min | Results Interpretation
Interpret the results based on statistical analysis. Determine if the trehalose group shows at least a 15 percentage point increase in viability compared to the DMSO group.
Expected outcome: Conclusions drawn regarding the efficacy of trehalose as a cryoprotectant.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Trehalose provides superior cryoprotection compared to DMSO for HeLa cells.
Post-thaw recovery time is sufficient for cell viability assessment.
Trypan blue exclusion accurately reflects cell viability.
decision_criteria: Proceed if trehalose group viability is at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO group.
Proceed if statistical analysis shows p < 0.05 for viability comparison.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot of HeLa cells for all experiments.
Standardize freezing and thawing rates across trials.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting
3. change one

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than the standard DMSO protocol improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with trehalose versus standard DMSO protocol.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
